report: Good evening, Michael. Here's the handover for Mr. John Doe, 68 years old, admitted yesterday with severe community-acquired pneumonia. - **Current Status:** He remains on high-flow nasal cannula (HFNC) at 45 L/min with FiO2 70%, maintaining SpO2 around 94-95%. He's showing some improvement but still requires close monitoring. - **Vital Signs:** At the end of my shift, his temperature is 37.5°C, heart rate is 100 bpm, blood pressure is 132/76 mmHg, respiratory rate is 22 breaths per minute, and SpO2 is 95% on HFNC. - **Assessments:** His respiratory effort has slightly improved throughout the day. Lung sounds still reveal crackles in the right lower lobe but no significant changes. He's alert, oriented, and communicating well. - **Medications:** He's on IV ceftriaxone and azithromycin for antibiotics. I gave him acetaminophen for fever earlier today, and his temperature has come down. No new medications were added during my shift. - **Lab Results:** Latest labs show WBC has come down a bit to 14,500/mm³. CRP is still elevated but showing a downward trend. ABG shows slight improvement with pH 7.34, pCO2 50 mmHg, and pO2 65 mmHg. - **Interventions:** We had to adjust his HFNC settings earlier when his SpO2 dropped briefly, but he's been stable since. I also assisted him with his meals; he’s tolerating a soft diet well and encouraged fluid intake. - **Family Communication:** I updated his spouse on his current status and addressed their concerns about his prognosis. They seemed reassured. - **Plan:** Keep a close eye on his respiratory status and make sure his SpO2 stays above 92%. Continue regular vital signs assessments and maintain his current HFNC settings unless he shows signs of respiratory distress. He's scheduled for a repeat chest X-ray in the next 24-48 hours to assess his response to treatment. Please watch for any changes in his respiratory effort, and don't hesitate to call the pulmonologist or infectious disease team if there are any concerns. Do you have any questions about his care or anything else I need to clarify?
response: No, that covers everything. Thanks, Anne. I'll keep a close watch on him through the night.
origin: ChatGPT, clinical medicine handbook (prompt: ICU patient with pneumonia)
